summary: Resplit Popo/Ice Climbers special-move code so the old broad ftPp_SpecialS.c unit became separate matching/linkable SpecialS, SpecialHi, and SpecialLw objects. The PR added new ftPp_SpecialHi.c and ftPp_SpecialLw.c files, moved declarations into move-specific headers, updated build/split/symbol config, and decomp.dev reported +14,416 linked-code bytes with 80 new matches.

key_files.role: Changed the ftPp_SpecialS text/sdata2 ranges and added separate ftPp_SpecialHi and ftPp_SpecialLw split entries.
Changed ftPp_SpecialS.c from NonMatching to Matching and added Matching objects for ftPp_SpecialHi.c and ftPp_SpecialLw.c.
Converted several former global address labels in this region to local @ symbols for strings and sdata2 constants.
Shrank to the side-special unit after moving Hi/Lw bodies out; includes were cleaned up accordingly.
New 932-line Popo up-special unit, including a documented sdata2 float-alignment/order hack.
New 212-line Popo down-special unit with Blizzard item/effect handling and Lw callbacks.
Renamed from the generic ftPp_1211.h and expanded with SpecialHi declarations.
Removed declarations that belonged to SpecialHi/SpecialLw.

what_changed: In splits.txt, ftPp_SpecialS.c text was shortened from 0x8011F68C-0x80122EDC to 0x8011F68C-0x80120E68, while ftPp_SpecialHi.c was added at 0x80120E68-0x80122898 and ftPp_SpecialLw.c at 0x80122898-0x80122EDC.
The old ftPp_SpecialS.c lost 1148 lines and now keeps the side-special portion instead of also carrying up-special and down-special code.
Added ftPp_SpecialHi.c with matched Popo up-special/start/throw/air variants plus helper functions such as ftPp_SpecialS_80120E68, ftPp_SpecialS_80120FE0, and ftPp_SpecialHi_8012280C.
Added ftPp_SpecialLw.c with matched Popo down-special entry, anim, phys, coll, and accessory callbacks including ftPp_SpecialLw_Coll, ftPp_SpecialAirLw_Coll, fn_80122B54, and fn_80122D2C.
Updated configure.py so ftPp_SpecialS.c is built as Matching and the new ftPp_SpecialHi.c and ftPp_SpecialLw.c are also Matching objects.
Renamed/reworked ftPp_1211.h into ftPp_SpecialHi.h, introduced ftPp_SpecialLw.h, and removed Hi/Lw prototypes from ftPp_SpecialS.h.
Updated include sites in ftCo_0A01.c, ftNn_Init.c, ftPp_Init.c, and ftdata.c to include ftPp_SpecialHi.h and ftPp_SpecialLw.h instead of the generic ftPp_1211.h.
symbols.txt changed multiple labels in the affected data/sdata/sdata2 region, including ftPp_Unk0_* and ftPp_Init_* constants, from global-style names to local @NNN symbols.

The decomp.dev bot reported linked code rising to 38.34% with +0.37% / +14,416 bytes, matched data +80 bytes, linked data +160 bytes, and 80 new matches; the listed broken matches were the old ftPp_SpecialS unit losing functions that moved to the new units.
smart_moves: The PR paired the source-file resplit with splits.txt boundaries, symbols.txt updates, and configure.py object status changes, so the new object layout could actually link instead of just reorganizing files.
It replaced the ambiguous ftPp_1211.h include with move-specific headers, which makes future Popo work search by SpecialHi/SpecialLw instead of by an address-derived placeholder name.
It moved Lw declarations out of the SpecialHi header and removed Hi/Lw declarations from SpecialS.h, reducing cross-object header leakage.
The symbols.txt changes avoided pretending anonymous constants and strings were meaningful globals; local @ symbols better match how these sdata/sdata2 objects are being emitted.
The author explicitly called out the sdata2_ordering float-alignment hack in a review comment, making the matching wart searchable and easier to replace later.
decomp_lessons: When a file contains multiple logical original objects, matching often requires fixing the split map first; otherwise function matches can appear under the wrong unit and cannot be linked cleanly.
A resplit can show many broken matches in the old unit while still being a net win if the same functions become new matches in correctly named units.
Header boundaries should follow object/move ownership: SpecialS.h should not be the dumping ground for SpecialHi and SpecialLw declarations.
Prefer descriptive move-specific file names such as ftPp_SpecialHi.h over address-derived placeholders such as ftPp_1211.h once the object identity is known.

.sdata2 float/double order and alignment can be fragile across split translation units; if a dummy ordering function is needed, isolate and document it so future decomp work can replace it with a cleaner alignment method.
For anonymous local constants, use local @NNN symbols in symbols.txt rather than inventing global names that imply cross-file API.
naming_conventions: Popo files use the ftPp_ prefix and move names: ftPp_SpecialS, ftPp_SpecialHi, and ftPp_SpecialLw.
Ground/air callback names follow the existing pattern: Enter, Anim, IASA, Phys, Coll, with Air variants such as ftPp_SpecialAirHi_Enter and ftPp_SpecialAirLw_Anim.
Unknown helper functions kept address-suffixed names such as ftPp_SpecialHi_8012280C and ftPp_SpecialS_80120E68 rather than inventing behavior names.
Truly unknown local functions in the new Lw unit still use fn_ address names, e.g. fn_80122B54 and fn_80122D2C.
Local data symbols in symbols.txt use @NNN labels when no semantic global name is justified.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Updated split boundaries exactly around 0x80120E68 and 0x80122898 so SpecialS, SpecialHi, and SpecialLw compile as separate matching objects.
Used a static sdata2_ordering function in ftPp_SpecialHi.c to force float/double constant ordering/alignment; the author called this a hack and asked for a better float-alignment method.
Used a custom inline my_sqrtf with __frsqrte refinement and a volatile float output pointer to match generated PowerPC sqrt-like code.
Kept PAD_STACK annotations and small static inline helpers to steer stack layout and inlining for matching.
Preserved quirks such as empty IASA functions and a goto path in ftPp_SpecialS_80120FE0 with a comment that the original returns without a value.
Set configure.py objects to Matching only after the source, headers, splits, and symbols were made consistent.
review_feedback: The PR body was empty in the slice; most intent comes from the title, diff, and bot report.

The only human review comment was by the author on ftPp_SpecialHi.c: "please forgive this hideous hack. If there's a better way to align the floats, I'm all ears!" This referred to the sdata2_ordering float/double constant-ordering helper.
The decomp.dev bot reported +14,416 linked-code bytes, +80 matched-data bytes, +160 linked-data bytes, and 80 new matches for GALE01.
follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for sdata2_ordering to see whether this float-alignment hack was replaced with a cleaner pattern.
Search for remaining ftPp_1211.h references or other address-derived Popo headers that should be renamed to move-specific headers.
Compare ftPp_SpecialHi and ftPp_SpecialLw with Nana/Ice Climbers counterpart files for shared helper naming and declarations.
Look for other resplit PRs where decomp.dev reports broken matches in an old unit but new matches in newly split units.
Audit local @NNN symbols in the Popo/Nana sdata2 region for constants that later gained justified semantic names.
